notes: South Korea is outside the EU/EEA/Switzerland: long stays are permit-driven, and an MVV (long-stay entry visa, often described as a Type D national visa sticker) is required in many—but not all—procedures; verify the MVV exemption list and your specific route on the IND.
Short-stay Schengen rules are a different process from relocating for work, study, or family.

MVV and residence permit applications are often linked from abroad; the IND decision letter indicates where to collect the MVV sticker, commonly via the Dutch mission in Seoul when that applies to you.

Netherlands Worldwide publishes South Korea–specific legalisation guidance—cross-check each document type.

seo.title: Moving to the Netherlands from South Korea | Visa, MVV, Documents & Practical Setup
seo.description: Planning a move from South Korea to the Netherlands? Explore visa routes, MVV guidance, document apostille requirements, housing, and the practical steps to settle in.
keywords: moving to the netherlands from south korea
move from south korea to netherlands
relocating from south korea to netherlands
netherlands long stay visa south korea

customNotes: South Korea–origin moves often hinge on apostille timing, Korean-to-Dutch/English translation work, and Seoul embassy appointment slots.

faqOverrides.q: Do South Korean nationals need a visa to visit the Netherlands?
faqOverrides.a: Short stays in the Schengen area follow Schengen short-stay rules. Netherlands Worldwide has South Korea–specific pages for Schengen visas and entry visas when one is required. Many South Korean passport holders can visit short-term without a Schengen visa under current rules, but purpose of stay and edge cases still matter—and short visits are not the same as relocating for work or study.

faqOverrides.q: Do I need an MVV to move from South Korea to the Netherlands?
faqOverrides.a: For stays longer than short visits, you normally need a residence permit for a recognised purpose. Many routes also require an MVV long-stay entry visa (a Type D visa sticker) before travel, but exemptions exist for some situations—check the IND’s MVV information and your specific permit type rather than assuming one answer for everyone.

faqOverrides.q: Where is the MVV collected when applying from South Korea?
faqOverrides.a: After a positive decision, the IND letter states where to collect the MVV sticker. Netherlands Worldwide describes MVV applications and appointments for South Korea; collection and appointments are often handled through the Dutch embassy in Seoul when that applies to your procedure—always follow the current official instructions.

faqOverrides.q: How do I legalise South Korean documents for Dutch authorities?
faqOverrides.a: Many South Korea–issued public documents need a Hague apostille through the competent South Korean authorities for use in the Netherlands, as explained on Netherlands Worldwide. Documents in Korean generally need translation into Dutch, English, French, or German for Dutch use. Confirm each requirement with the body that requests the document.

faqOverrides.q: What about the civic integration exam abroad?
faqOverrides.a: Depending on your route, civic integration requirements may apply. Netherlands Worldwide lists South Korea–specific information for the civic integration exam abroad when relevant to your situation.

faqOverrides.q: What should I plan for immediately after arriving?
faqOverrides.a: Most people prioritise municipal registration and BSN, residence permit steps if applicable, a bank account, and Dutch basic health insurance when resident. DigiD and GP registration follow once identifiers exist.
No. This guide summarises common planning themes and links to official sources. Always confirm your situation with the IND, Netherlands Worldwide, your sponsor, or qualified advisors.

scenarioOverrides.title: South Korean professional relocating for a Dutch job
scenarioOverrides.summary: Coordinate sponsor timelines, MVV collection per your IND letter (often via Seoul when applicable), housing within commute range, and BSN-first banking and insurance.

scenarioOverrides.title: Student moving from South Korea to a Dutch university
scenarioOverrides.summary: Match admission to study permit conditions; plan apostilles and certified translations on academic and civil documents; budget for student housing.

scenarioOverrides.title: Partner or family relocation from South Korea
scenarioOverrides.summary: Relationship evidence and civil documents are central; timelines depend on your partner’s status in the Netherlands.

scenarioOverrides.title: Entrepreneur exploring startup or self-employment
scenarioOverrides.summary: Map IND criteria, possible facilitator involvement, and municipality registration—expect more bespoke evidence than a standard employee route.

scenarioOverrides.title: Corporate transfer from a South Korea office
scenarioOverrides.summary: Clarify sponsor, permit type, MVV sequencing, and payroll transition with your mobility team.
